name: Robert's Western World
best_time: 11am-3pm for Brazilbilly, late night for chaos
insider_tip: Order the recession special: fried bologna sandwich, chips, Moon Pie, PBR — $6. Real country music, not top-40. Cash tips in the jar keep the band playing.

name: Tootsies Orange Lounge
insider_tip: Go upstairs — third floor has the best band and the least sorority crowd. Skip the front door line and enter from the Ryman alley.

name: The Stage on Broadway
insider_tip: Only spot on Broadway with a legit dance floor. Get there by 7:30 for the good band slot at 8.

name: Acme Feed & Seed
insider_tip: Rooftop at sunset is the best free view of the Cumberland. Skip the first-floor food, head straight up.

name: Ole Red
insider_tip: Rooftop view rivals Acme. Reservations required weekends — book 2 weeks out. Food is better than you'd expect from a celebrity bar.

name: Prince's Hot Chicken Shack
insider_tip: The ORIGINAL hot chicken — older than Hattie B's by 80 years. Order medium if you've never had it. 'Hot' will end you. Expect a wait, it's worth it.

name: Hattie B's Hot Chicken
insider_tip: The famous one. Line is always long — go between lunch and dinner. Start at 'medium' then try 'hot' if you're brave. The pimento mac is the play.

name: Bolton's Spicy Chicken & Fish
insider_tip: Where locals actually go. Cash only at the East Main spot. Order the half chicken hot, white bread, dill pickles. Done.

name: The Bluebird Cafe
insider_tip: THE songwriter room. The 6pm 'early show' is a sit-down audition round and way better than the 9pm. Reservations open at 8am on Monday for the following week — set an alarm. SHHHHH is mandatory.

name: Station Inn
insider_tip: Cash only, no reservations, arrive 90 min early. This is where the best bluegrass players in the world show up unannounced. Sunday night jam is free.

name: The Listening Room Cafe
insider_tip: The Bluebird without the impossible reservations. 4 writers in the round, real food, decent drinks. Book 2 weeks out for a weekend.

name: Puckett's Grocery & Restaurant
insider_tip: Meat-and-three done right downtown. Saturday brunch has live bluegrass. Order the fried catfish and mac.

name: Biscuit Love
insider_tip: Get the Bonuts (biscuit donuts, lemon mascarpone). Line is brutal on weekends — weekday mornings are the move. No reservations.

name: Loveless Cafe
insider_tip: 25 min out of downtown but worth it for the biscuits and preserves. Order the country ham and extra biscuits to go. Weekends = 2hr wait.

name: The Catbird Seat
insider_tip: Chef's counter, 9-course tasting menu. Book 30 days out the second reservations open. Worth every penny for the splurge trip.

name: Husk Nashville
insider_tip: In a restored 19th century house. Everything on the menu comes from within 100 miles. Order the cornbread and whatever pig dish is on the board.

name: Rolf and Daughters
insider_tip: Best pasta in Nashville, period. The garganelli verdi is non-negotiable. Book 2-3 weeks out.

name: Henrietta Red
insider_tip: Best oyster bar in town. Happy hour oysters are a steal. Get the gem salad and whatever fish is on special.

name: Arnold's Country Kitchen
insider_tip: Cafeteria line, James Beard winner. Closed weekends and after 2:45pm. Roast beef, turnip greens, mac, cornbread, chess pie — full board.

name: Monell's
insider_tip: Sit at a table with strangers, pass the bowls. Unlimited fried chicken, skillet-fried, plus 8 sides. Go hungry.

name: Ryman Auditorium
address: 116 Rep. John Lewis Way N, Nashville, TN 37219
insider_tip: The Mother Church of Country Music. Take the backstage tour in the day OR catch a show at night (or both). Pew seats hurt after 2 hours — bring a cushion.

name: Country Music Hall of Fame
address: 222 Rep. John Lewis Way S, Nashville, TN 37203
insider_tip: Budget 3 hours. The Hatch Show Print tour is the hidden gem — oldest letterpress poster shop in America, on-site.

name: Johnny Cash Museum
insider_tip: Small but incredible — the best single-artist museum in town. 60-90 min. Combo ticket with Patsy Cline Museum upstairs saves $5.

name: Grand Ole Opry
insider_tip: Worth the Uber out. Saturday night shows are the real Opry. Mix of legends and new artists, 2 hours, no cell phones. Book the backstage tour add-on.

name: Centennial Park (Parthenon)
insider_tip: Full-scale replica of the Athens Parthenon. Walk around it for free, or $10 to go inside and see the 42-foot Athena statue. Killer photo spot.

name: Percy Warner Park
insider_tip: Best hiking close to the city. The 4.5mi Mossy Ridge loop is the move. Go early — parking fills by 9am on weekends.

name: Shelby Bottoms Greenway
insider_tip: 5 miles of paved greenway along the Cumberland. Rent a bike from Halcyon Bike Shop and ride it. Flat, easy, and you can end at a brewery.

name: Radnor Lake State Park
insider_tip: Deer, herons, owls, 15 min from downtown. Lake Trail is flat and 2 miles. No pets, no running — it's a protected area.

name: Barista Parlor
insider_tip: Third-wave coffee in a converted garage. Order a Golden Sound (espresso, vanilla, milk). The Germantown location is prettier.

name: Frothy Monkey
insider_tip: Reliable brunch coffee spot on 12 South. Good for working. The biscuit sandwich is the move.

name: Crema Coffee Roasters
insider_tip: Best espresso in the city, full stop. Roasted on-site. Tiny spot, no attitude.

name: Five Points (East Nashville)
insider_tip: The heart of cool East Nashville. Walk from I Dream of Weenie to Rosepepper Cantina to Pied Piper Creamery, then a pint at 3 Crow Bar.

name: 12 South
insider_tip: The 'I Believe in Nashville' mural is here. Walkable strip of boutiques (Draper James, White's Mercantile) and coffee. Tourist-heavy but legit.

name: The Gulch
insider_tip: Upscale neighborhood with the 'What Lifts You' wings mural. Dinner at Rolf and Daughters, drinks at L.A. Jackson rooftop after.

name: Germantown
insider_tip: Nashville's oldest neighborhood, best dining scene (Rolf, Henrietta Red, Butchertown Hall). Walkable and quieter than Broadway.

name: Music Row
insider_tip: Drive down 16th and 17th Ave to see the studios where country music got made. RCA Studio B (tours via Hall of Fame) is the highlight.

name: L.A. Jackson
insider_tip: Rooftop of the Thompson Hotel. Skyline view, real cocktails. Go at sunset, avoid peak Saturday.

name: Attaboy
insider_tip: No menu, no sign. Tell the bartender three spirits or flavors you like, they build you a drink. Unmarked door, ring the bell.

name: Pinewood Social
insider_tip: Bowling alley + coffee shop + pool + restaurant in a converted trolley barn. Reserve a lane ahead. The fried chicken sandwich slaps.

name: Dino's
insider_tip: The dive bar locals actually love. Cheeseburger and a shot is under $15. Open until 3am.

name: Santa's Pub
insider_tip: Trailer bar. Cash only. Best karaoke in Nashville by a mile. Santa himself runs it. Arrive before 10pm or the list is closed.

name: Fran's East Side
insider_tip: Cheap beer, good jukebox, no attitude. Perfect starter bar on an East Nashville crawl.

name: Urban Cowboy Public House
insider_tip: In the back of a boutique hotel. Best patio in East Nashville. Order the Bourbon Renewal.

name: Nashville Farmers' Market
address: 900 Rosa L Parks Blvd, Nashville, TN 37208
insider_tip: Free to walk around, food hall inside has Jamaicaway (real jerk chicken) and B&C BBQ. Good rainy-day move.

name: Assembly Food Hall
insider_tip: 30+ vendors, rooftop bar on top. Good for groups with different cravings. Prince's Hot Chicken has a stall here if you can't make it to Nolensville.

name: Jeni's Splendid Ice Creams
insider_tip: Brambleberry Crisp and Brown Butter Almond Brittle. Get the two-scoop trio so you can try three flavors.

name: Five Daughters Bakery
insider_tip: 100-layer donuts. Get the maple glazed and the key lime. They sell out by 11am.

name: Martin's Bar-B-Que Joint
insider_tip: Whole hog BBQ, the real deal. Order the Redneck Taco (cornbread hoecake topped with pulled pork) and white sauce chicken.

name: Peg Leg Porker
insider_tip: Dry-rub ribs, Memphis-style. Order the ribs and the smoked bologna. Cash only or card, but they take neither checks.

name: Edley's Bar-B-Que
insider_tip: The Tuck Special (brisket, pulled pork, red sauce, slaw, pickle on top) is the order. Good patio.

name: Hatch Show Print
address: 224 Rep. John Lewis Way S, Nashville, TN 37203
insider_tip: Oldest working letterpress poster shop in America (1879). Tour is $20, shop is free. Best souvenir you can buy in Nashville.

name: Third Man Records
insider_tip: Jack White's label HQ. Record yourself in the 1940s booth for $20. The Blue Room hosts surprise shows — check the calendar.

name: RCA Studio B
insider_tip: Elvis cut 260 songs here. Tour only via Country Music Hall of Fame shuttle. Book the combo ticket.

name: Printers Alley
insider_tip: Old speakeasy alley between Church and Union. Skull's Rainbow Room has burlesque + live music most nights. Tucked away from Broadway chaos.

name: Honky Tonk Highway (Broadway walking tour)
insider_tip: Start at 5th & Broadway, walk down. Pop into 3 bars on the way: Robert's, Legends Corner, Layla's. One drink each, different band at each. 2 hours.
